name: Ensure ECS clusters use Container Insights
body:
### Security objective

This control ensures that **Amazon ECS clusters emit Container Insights telemetry**.

### Assessment detail

The ECS cluster settings are expected to include containerInsights with the value enabled. This is a direct configuration check on the cluster.

### Operational value

Container Insights gives platform and security teams workload-level visibility into services, tasks, CPU, memory, networking, and lifecycle behavior. Without this telemetry, incident response and capacity troubleshooting become slower and more dependent on application-side logs.

Production and internet-facing ECS clusters should have this enabled by default so abnormal behavior can be investigated from CloudWatch without first changing the cluster configuration during an incident.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

### AWS

Enable Amazon ECS Container Insights on the affected cluster. The check verifies the ECS cluster settings for containerInsights=enabled.

1. Open **Amazon ECS > Clusters**.
2. Select the affected cluster.
3. Choose **Update cluster**.
4. Enable **Container Insights**.
5. Save the cluster configuration.
6. Confirm CloudWatch starts receiving Container Insights metrics for the cluster.

AWS CLI:

~~~bash
aws ecs update-cluster-settings \
  --cluster {{asset.name}} \
  --settings name=containerInsights,value=enabled \
  --region {{asset.region}}
~~~

Validate the setting:

~~~bash
aws ecs describe-clusters \
  --clusters {{asset.name}} \
  --include SETTINGS \
  --region {{asset.region}} \
  --query 'clusters[0].settings'
~~~

References:
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/deploy-container-insights-ECS-cluster.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/ecs/update-cluster-settings.html

The control is satisfied when the ECS cluster has a setting named containerInsights with value enabled.
description: Amazon ECS clusters that do not have Container Insights enabled.
